vehicle.label: 6-DOF Industrial Arm
vehicle.desc: 6-DOF serial industrial robot arm, 5kg payload, 600mm reach

parts.label: Base Joint (J1)
parts.spec: Base rotary joint J1, +-180deg, harmonic drive, 100Nm peak torque, mounting flange ISO 9409

parts.label: Shoulder Joint (J2)
parts.spec: Shoulder pitch joint J2, +-120deg, harmonic drive, 80Nm, cable routing through center bore

parts.label: Upper Arm (Link 2-3)
parts.spec: Upper arm link, 300mm length, hollow tube with cable conduit, lightweight Al 7075

parts.label: Elbow Joint (J3)
parts.spec: Elbow pitch joint J3, +-150deg, cycloidal reducer, 50Nm, encoder dual-stack

parts.label: Forearm + Wrist (J4)
parts.spec: Forearm with wrist roll J4, 250mm length, +-360deg continuous, slip ring for power/signal

parts.label: Wrist Pitch (J5)
parts.spec: Wrist pitch joint J5, +-120deg, harmonic drive, 20Nm, compact form factor

parts.label: Wrist Roll (J6)
parts.spec: Wrist final roll joint J6, +-360deg, harmonic drive, 10Nm, ISO 9409-1 50mm tool flange

parts.label: Tool Flange + Changer
parts.spec: Tool changer master plate at J6 end, pneumatic locking, 4 utility ports

parts.label: Cable Management Spine
parts.spec: External cable carrier from base to wrist, drag chain or spiral, signal + power + pneumatic

parts.label: Replaceable Motor Module Set
parts.spec: Actuation_motors closure module: replaceable motor_module cartridges for J1-J6, fastener_access, coupling_dummy, insert/washer preload pads, torque_Nm intent labels

parts.label: Encoder Cover + Sensing Set
parts.spec: Sensing_encoders closure module: encoder_pocket and encoder_cover at each joint, index marks, cable exit strain relief, inspection labels

parts.label: Drive Electronics Bay
parts.spec: Drive_electronics bay with driver_bay, power_channel, signal_channel, ground_point, service cover, connector keying, thermal inspection window

requirements: Base rotary joint J1, +-180deg, harmonic drive, 100Nm peak torque, mounting flange ISO 9409
Vehicle context: 6-DOF serial industrial robot arm, 5kg payload, 600mm reach
Application variant: welding cell deployment, heat-shielded links, spark-resistant cable routing
Suggested material: Al 7075 + steel + composite, process: CNC + LPBF

risk.desc: Potential for harmonic drive overheating due to insufficient heat dissipation.
risk.mit: Increase heat sink area or add active cooling.

risk.desc: Risk of cable damage due to excessive strain relief.
risk.mit: Review and adjust strain relief geometry.

risk.desc: Possible misalignment during assembly due to unclear leveling mounting holes.
risk.mit: Add clear alignment marks and improve inspection readability.

risk.desc: Risk of structural failure due to insufficient rib reinforcement.
risk.mit: Increase rib thickness or add additional ribs.

risk.desc: Potential for service access panel to interfere with cable routing.
risk.mit: Adjust service access panel position or redesign cable routing.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Base rotary joint J1, +-180deg, harmonic drive, 100Nm peak torque, mounting flange ISO 9409

physics_paths.path_description: Transmits torque and rotational forces from the base joint to the robot arm body.
Transmits electrical and data signals from the base joint to the robot arm body.
Transfers heat generated by the harmonic drive to the heat sink for dissipation.
Enables 360-degree rotation of the robot arm body around the base joint.
Provides access for maintenance and inspection of the base joint.

adjacent_interfaces.assembly_order_hint: Install robot arm body after base joint is aligned and secured.
Route cables through the base joint before securing the robot arm body.
Install leveling system after base joint is aligned.

risk.desc: Potential thermal stress due to high heat dissipation from harmonic drive.
risk.mit: Implement thermal management strategies such as heat sinks and cooling channels.

risk.desc: Risk of cable damage due to tight routing through center bore.
risk.mit: Ensure adequate slack in harness routing and use strain relief at entry/exit points.

risk.desc: Risk of misalignment during assembly due to complex bolt pattern.
risk.mit: Provide detailed assembly instructions and alignment tools.

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient service access for maintenance.
risk.mit: Increase the size of service access cuts and ensure they are easily accessible.

risk.desc: Risk of material fatigue due to high cycle life.
risk.mit: Select materials with high fatigue strength and incorporate stress relief features.

risk.desc: Potential thermal expansion issues due to the large temperature range (10-40C).
risk.mit: Implement thermal expansion joints and use materials with similar coefficients of thermal expansion.

risk.desc: Risk of cable damage due to the welding environment's heat and sparks.
risk.mit: Use heat-resistant and spark-proof cable materials and ensure proper routing and protection.

risk.desc: Risk of alignment issues during assembly due to the complexity of the joint interfaces.
risk.mit: Include detailed assembly instructions and alignment aids in the assembly_sequence.

risk.desc: Potential for fastener failure if preload is not properly applied.
risk.mit: Ensure that all fasteners are torqued to the correct preload specification.

risk.desc: Risk of structural failure due to inadequate ribbing or stiffening.
risk.mit: Conduct a thorough FEA analysis to validate the ribbing and stiffening design.

Maintenance personnel can access the joint for service and inspection.

risk.desc: Potential misalignment during assembly due to lack of datum reference.
risk.mit: Add a primary datum feature (flat, bore, or pin) and ensure it is explicitly named and placed.

risk.desc: Cable harnesses may not have adequate strain relief geometry.
risk.mit: Ensure every cable exit shows strain relief geometry (clamp, grommet, tie point) and routing respects a stated minimum bend radius.

risk.desc: Potential for mis-mating connectors due to lack of keying or labeling.
risk.mit: Ensure connectors are reachable without disassembling structure and have keying or labeling to prevent mis-mating.

risk.desc: Risk of cable routing being buried inside closed links without inspection features.
risk.mit: Ensure cable routing is externally inspectable or through open channels.

risk.desc: Potential for bearing dummies to float in links without proper mounting features.
risk.mit: Ensure each joint shows a bearing seat with shoulder or retaining feature, shaft locating geometry, and stated fit intent for inner/outer races.
